channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: New Proposal Would Allow Administration to Block Grants if They Don’t Support Trump’s Agenda
item.description: A new proposal would allow the administration to block grants if they do not satisfy President Trump’s agenda or support what it calls “anti-American” values.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.media:description: President Trump’s ambitions to impose new political criteria on grants were made clear in a roughly 400-page blueprint that was released on Friday.

item.title: Trump Names Bill Pulte as Acting Director of National Intelligence
item.description: Bill Pulte, a Trump loyalist who leads the federal housing finance agency, will oversee the U.S. intelligence agencies. He has no known background in intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman, Julian E. Barnes and Matthew Goldstein
item.media:description: Bill Pulte, now the director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, during his confirmation hearing in Washington last year.

item.title: Trump Signs Executive Order Seeking Oversight of A.I. Models
item.description: The order, which signaled a shift from the hands-off approach the White House had previously taken toward A.I., followed debates over how to gain control of A.I. models without disrupting innovation.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel and Tripp Mickle
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House last week.

item.title: Polls of California Governor’s Race Show a Clear Top 3
item.description: Xavier Becerra, Steve Hilton and Tom Steyer have consistently led the field in California’s nonpartisan primary, where the top two finishers advance.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler

item.title: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Iowa, Montana and Beyond
item.description: Democrats have a competitive Senate primary race in Iowa, where the party is hoping to make gains, while a smokejumper is running in a contested House primary in Montana.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Rachel Mummey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voting on Tuesday in Des Moines.

item.title: Only the Right Tests Can Stop This Ebola Outbreak. Congo Has Hardly Any.
item.description: A chronic lack of investment in development of better tests has left clinicians blind and allows deadly viruses to spread unchecked.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nolen
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Health workers suiting up in Mongbwalu, in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Without sufficient testing, people with symptoms that could be Ebola are often isolated together.

item.title: U.S. Ebola Unit Plans in Kenya, Subject of Protests, Suffers New Setback From Court Ruling
item.description: The court further delayed the Trump administration’s proposed quarantine unit for Americans exposed to the virus. The plan has sparked angry protests in Kenya.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg and Brian O. Otieno
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Nanyuki, Kenya, on Monday, protesting plans to create an Ebola quarantine center for Americans.

item.title: How Three Chess Friends Battled Demons and Saved Two Lives
item.description: A homeless chess hustler, a scholar and an aging recluse found hope in a trashed apartment by Central Park.
item.dc:creator: John Leland and Juan Arredondo
item.media:description: Chess was what drew the friends together — the same rules and chances for everyone who played.

item.title: Bombs Raining Down on Ukraine Can’t Hide Signs of Russia’s Faltering War
item.description: The war has not been going the Kremlin’s way, with battleground losses and growing casualties. With fiercer strikes, Moscow hopes to gain a better position for negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.media:description: Smoke rising from buildings in Kyiv on Tuesday, after a Russian missile and drone attack.

item.title: Russia Launches Deadly Strikes on Kyiv After Threatening Ukraine for a Week
item.description: Moscow’s repeated warnings of a major strike, combined with the delay before it happened, seemed intended to inflict a psychological toll on the Ukrainian capital.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova and Kim Barker
item.media:description: The aftermath of a Russian strike on a residential building in Kyiv, Ukraine’s capital, on Tuesday.

item.title: The Toll of a ‘Missing Scientists’ Conspiracy Theory on the Families Left Behind
item.description: The claim that their loved ones’ deaths and disappearances are linked is almost certainly false — but the loss remains real.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Nazaryan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Rebecca Noble for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elizabeth Weiss, an anthropologist whose husband, Nick Pope, a prominent U.F.O. researcher, died in April. “If you start looking for patterns, you will find them,” she said, quoting him.

item.title: Obama Center’s Two Sides: A Lovely Park and a Forbidding Tower
item.description: In Chicago, the $850 million Obama Presidential Center aims to remake a neighborhood with a 19.3-acre community hub and a brooding 225-foot museum.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kimmelman and Lyndon French
item.category: Obama, Barack H, Presidential Library and Museum
item.media:credit: Lyndon French for The New York Times

item.title: What Ireland and Germany Can Teach Us About Birthright Citizenship
item.description: President Trump has argued that the United States is “stupid” for granting citizenship at birth. Most countries don’t do so, but that can create problems.
item.dc:creator: Lydia DePillis, Megan Specia and Tatiana Firsova
item.media:credit: Ellius Grace for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mariam Sobayo was born in Dublin in February 2005, a few weeks after the country halted its longstanding practice of granting citizenship to all children born on Irish soil.

item.title: Carney Says Canada Faces a ‘Crisis of Antisemitism’
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s comments followed calls for action from Jewish organizations and after a government report found that antisemitism had surged in Canada in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.media:credit: Chris Young/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada speaking at Holy Blossom Temple in Toronto on Monday.

item.title: ‘La La Land’ Orchestral Performance Saved by Keyboardist in the Audience
item.description: After a musician fell ill during a live performance of the score from “La La Land,” the composer Justin Hurwitz asked for a sight reader. A 21-year-old student stepped up.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses

item.title: Netanyahu’s Critics Accuse Him of Running Israel as a U.S. ‘Vassal State’
item.description: Critics pounced on the Israeli prime minister after President Trump claimed on social media he had persuaded him to call off “a major raid” on Hezbollah in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel has faced criticism from his opponents after he appeared to call off an attack on Hezbollah in the Lebanese capital.

item.title: In Her Memoir, Jill Biden Is a Watchful Spouse Who Didn’t Always Speak Up About Joe Biden
item.description: The former first lady’s new book reflects an insular White House where loyalty was prized and President Joseph R. Biden Jr.’s feelings were prioritized over health concerns.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.media:description: Jill Biden in 2022. In her telling, by the time she got a glimpse of what others had feared about her husband as president, it was too late.

item.title: Book Review: ‘View From the East Wing,’ by Jill Biden
item.description: Beyond a few pointed digs at her husband’s successor, “View From the East Wing” largely sticks to the head-spinning details of first lady-hood.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Jacobs
item.category: View from the East Wing: A Memoir (Book)
item.media:description: Jill Biden returning to the White House in January 2025, weeks before her husband finished his term as president.

item.title: Trump Megadonor Gave $5.5 Million Estate to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Anti-Vaccine Group
item.description: A Mellon heir transferred the adjacent Connecticut properties, covering about 300 acres, at no cost to Children’s Health Defense last year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Davis O’Brien and Theodore Schleifer
item.media:description: Timothy Mellon in 1981. During the 2024 election cycle, he donated $150 million to a Trump super PAC and $25 million to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s super PAC.

item.title: A Stray Dog Became a Cultural Symbol in Brazil. Now, Mexico Is Claiming It.
item.description: To Brazilians, the ubiquitous caramel-colored mutts are a national icon. But a Mexican state has adopted them as its own homegrown symbol.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova and María Magdalena Arréllaga
item.media:description: Tiana, a “caramelo,” at a park in Rio de Janeiro in April.

item.title: Trump Has Failed as Commander in Chief
item.description: Trump has failed to unite the country while at war and instead is seeking personal gain.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Naila Ruechel for The New York Times. Source Photographs by Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times and Getty Images.

item.title: Dear President Ozymandias
item.description: Look on his works and despair.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.media:credit: Eli Hiller / AFP via Getty Images

item.title: Curfew to Be Lifted Near NJ’s Delaney Hall ICE Facility Where Protests Have Raged
item.description: The restrictions outside Delaney Hall immigration detention center were imposed after demonstrators who were protesting the conditions at the facility had clashed with the police.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
item.media:description: Demonstrators gathered on Monday outside the Delaney Hall immigration detention center in Newark.
